title: Foreign-status derivation blindness — mechanism, per-surface class inventory, and resolution shapes for cross-substrate consumption
question: Does pi-context's derived state consume foreign (cross-substrate) edge endpoints — and if not, where exactly does each derivation surface drop them, what is the blast radius, and what fix shapes does the shipped architecture afford?
method: Fresh-agent upstream verification of a downstream two-substrate report: every claim re-derived against current source (v0.32.0 + Unreleased, HEAD aafc8dc); two registered scratchpad substrates driven through built dist via the sanctioned porcelain (appendRelationByRef with alias:refname selectors, currentState, validateContext); per-surface code trace of every edge-consuming join; prior-art sweep of framework-gaps/features/decisions/research; design-record archaeology of the 2026-05-31 identity spec.
findings_summary: Every edge-consuming derivation surface resolves endpoints via endpointKey (bare refname, substrate_id discarded) against the ACTIVE substrate's byRefname index only, while the write path and validateContext's edge-integrity accept and certify foreign endpoints as valid — a split-brain with zero diagnostics on any derivation path. Per-surface polarity: gate/dependency satisfaction silently drops foreign blockers (a task gated on an OPEN foreign item derives ready and enters nextActions — empirically reproduced, validate clean even with a covering status-consistency invariant declared); derivedRollupComplete returns false for foreign members (a milestone with every member complete derives planned; reconcile/converge STAMP that false status durably); the status-consistency invariant skips foreign targets; requires-edge satisfaction never resolves the counter-endpoint; joinBlocks and roadmap rollups drop foreign counterparts; walks erase the substrate qualifier. Aggravator: foreign keys are bare refnames, so a same-refname local item (the expected case across catalog-installed substrates) silently substitutes its status for the foreign item's — reproduced: a local closed gB satisfied a gate pointing at foreign-open gB. findReferences is the only non-blind edge-read surface. The claimed single seam is partially true: derivedRollupComplete converges its three consumers, but gates, invariants, joinBlocks, roadmap, and walks carry independent active-index joins — the real seam is the endpointKey+byRefname join convention; resolveRef (registry-aware, per-pass cached, shipped v0.28.0) exists and is wired only into validation. The 2026-05-31 identity spec designed §J3 foreign-aware status-consistency (compare-if-readable, warn-if-unreadable — never implemented) and contains no derivation design. Resolution shapes the architecture affords: resolve-at-derive (registry resolution at every join, recommended), derive-as-blocked-until-resolved, flagged-exclusion floor — a genuine DEC-shaped semantics choice (freshness/cost/offline trade-offs); in every variant silent dropping and refname-collision aliasing must be eliminated and the dangling-vs-foreign conflation split.

revisions: workflowsPiExtension main HEAD aafc8dc at verification; dist built 2026-07-06 08:12, current with source

stale_conditions: Any change to normalizeEndpoint/endpointKey in packages/pi-context/src/context.ts (the foreign-key derivation and the collision-aliasing root)
Any change to the gate/dependency filters at packages/pi-context/src/context-sdk.ts incompletePreds/unsatisfiedGates or to derivedRollupComplete's membership lookup
FEAT-012 reaching implementation (any surface beginning to resolve foreign endpoints through the registry supersedes the per-surface inventory)

citations.label: gate filters + derivedRollupComplete + status-consistency skip + edge-integrity resolveRef loop + joinBlocks
reconcile/converge stamping via the shared completeness helper
findings document: reproduction transcripts, class inventory, prior-art sweep, resolution shapes
